_comment: DAQ configuration for prad2decoder — JLab single-event mode (tag 0xB1)

event_tags._comment: CODA2/JLab legacy control tags (confirmed in PRad-II data). Built-in physics trigger 0xFF50 - 0xFF8F and 0x00A0 - 0x00BF auto-detected. Monitoring tags have TI data but no waveforms — skipped in file viewer and iteration.

bank_tags._comment: JLab single-event mode banks. CODA trigger bank (0xFF2X) auto-detected by tag range.

bank_structure._comment: Three-level physics event hierarchy: event_bank (depth 0) → ROC_crate (depth 1) → data_bank (depth 2). Composite bank 0xE101 adds a 4th internal level (0x000D metadata + 0x0000 data words) consumed directly by its decoder. Each data bank maps to a decoder 'module' and a 'product'; EvChannel::Get<product>() lazily dispatches the registered module on first access and caches the result per event.

fadc250_waveform._comment: FADC250 waveform analysis parameters. 'firmware' configures the Fadc250FwAnalyzer (on-board Mode 1/2/3 emulator) used for DAQ signal studies; 'analyzer' configures the offline soft analyzer (fdec::WaveAnalyzer) that writes pedestal+peaks to the replay tree and feeds the viewer. Both sections are optional — absent fields keep the C++ defaults (see Fadc250FwConfig in DaqConfig.h and WaveConfig in WaveAnalyzer.h).

firmware._comment: Hall-D V3 firmware API (faV3HallDSetProcMode); NPEAK is accepted as an alias for MAX_PULSES. TET = Trigger Energy Threshold in ADC counts above per-channel pedestal. NSB / NSA = window before / after threshold crossing in ns (floored to whole samples = CLK_NS). NPEAK / MAX_PULSES = pulses kept per channel per window (1..4). NSAT = consecutive samples above TET required for a valid pulse (1 = legacy single-sample crossing). NPED = leading samples summed for Vnoise (firmware: 4..15; default 4 reproduces legacy manual). MAXPED = pedestal-subtracted threshold above which samples are excluded from the pedestal sum (online outlier rejection; 0 disables). CLK_NS = sample period in ns (4 ns at 250 MHz). Values below match the real DAQ run settings.

analyzer._comment: fdec::WaveConfig — offline soft analyzer. smooth_order: triangular kernel order (1 = identity, N gives a 2N-1 tap kernel). threshold / min_threshold: peak threshold in ped-RMS units, with absolute floor in ADC counts above pedestal. min_peak_ratio: a new peak must be ≥ this fraction of a nearby peak. int_tail_ratio: stop integration when signal drops below this fraction of peak height. tail_break_n: require N consecutive sub-threshold samples to terminate integration. peak_pileup_gap: peaks with integration bounds within this many samples are flagged Q_PEAK_PILED. ped_nsamples: max samples for the pedestal window. ped_flatness: max RMS for a 'flat' pedestal region (floors the noise estimate). ped_max_iter: σ-clip iterations for outlier rejection. overflow: ADC overflow value (12-bit FADC = 4095). clk_mhz: sample-clock frequency for time conversion.

nnls_deconv._comment: NNLS pile-up deconvolution. When enabled, the analyzer follows the local-maxima peak finder with a per-channel two-tau template fit that solves for non-negative per-peak amplitudes, recovering height_dec / integral_dec on piled events. Channels without a usable template fall back to the WaveAnalyzer values unless fallback_to_global_template is true. See docs/technical_notes/waveform_analysis/wave_analysis.md.

run_info._comment: 0xE10F bank in TI master crate (tag=0x27): run metadata

trigger_bank._comment: 0xC000 bank: 3 UINT32 words per event (event_number, event_tag, reserved)

ti_format._comment: TI bank (0xE10A, after rol2 stripping): d[0]=event_header (event_type in bits 31:24 = TS input pattern), d[1]=trigger#, d[2]=ts_low, d[3]=ts_high|evtnum_high. TI master d[5] = 32-bit FP trigger inputs (tiSetFPInputReadout enabled). Baseline TS pattern always extracted from d[0] bits 31:24; TI master d[5] overrides with richer 32-bit FP data.

dsc_scaler._comment: DSC2 scaler bank — used for measured DAQ livetime (1 - gated/ungated). Disabled until slot/channel are confirmed: leave bank_tag<0 or slot<0. When enabled, source ∈ 'ref' (125 MHz reference clock — time-based livetime), 'trg' (TRG input), 'tdc' (TDC input); channel 0..15 (ignored for ref).

sync_format._comment: Absolute-time / run-state banks used by EvChannel::Sync(). The HEAD bank (0xE112, 5 words) is carried inside SYNC/EPICS events; control events (PRESTART 0x0011, GO 0x0012, END 0x0014) carry a 3-word UINT32 payload with a different word layout. See prad2dec/include/SyncData.h for the decoded struct.
